is a Christian retreat center in [Harvey Cedars, New Jersey](/wiki/Harvey_Cedars,_New_Jersey "Harvey Cedars, New Jersey"), providing conferences, retreats, and other events. The Bible Conference has a long history of biblically oriented ministries and vacations for families and people of all ages and ethnicities.

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [39°41′47″N 74°08′39″W﻿ / ﻿39.69625°N 74.14425°W﻿ / 39.69625; -74.14425](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Harvey_Cedars_Bible_Conference&params=39.69625_N_74.14425_W_)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Church-owned complex in New Jersey, United States

Harvey Cedars Bible Conference

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Formerly | Harvey Cedars Presbyterian Bible Conference |
| Industry | Christian retreats |
| Founded | August 1, 1941; 84 years ago (1941-08-01) |
| Founder | [John W. Murray](/wiki/John_W._Murray "John W. Murray") |
| Headquarters | [Harvey Cedars, New Jersey](/wiki/Harvey_Cedars,_New_Jersey "Harvey Cedars, New Jersey") |
| Key people | Jonathan Oldham (Executive Director) |
| Website | [hcbible.org](https://www.hcbible.org) |

**Harvey Cedars Bible Conference** is a Christian retreat center in [Harvey Cedars, New Jersey](/wiki/Harvey_Cedars,_New_Jersey "Harvey Cedars, New Jersey"), providing conferences, retreats, and other events. The Bible Conference has a long history of biblically oriented ministries and vacations for families and people of all ages and ethnicities.

Summers feature week-long family and teen conferences. The rest of the year is filled with retreats for different organizations, including at least one retreat which brings in hundreds from other countries.

Lodging facilities include a [Victorian](/wiki/Victorian_era "Victorian era") hotel, a modern hotel, a motel, and other dormitory-style housing units. Other facilities include a dining hall, a [dock](/wiki/Dock_(maritime) "Dock (maritime)") and gazebo, a tennis court, an indoor heated swimming pool, an indoor hot tub, a gymnasium, volleyball courts, and a soccer field.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harvey_Cedars_Bible_Conference&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Harvey Cedars Bible Conference (called Harvey Cedars Bible Presbyterian Conference until 1949)[[1]](#cite_note-1) has occupied the former, historic [Harvey Cedars Hotel](/wiki/Harvey_Cedars_Hotel "Harvey Cedars Hotel") since August 3, 1941.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Presbyterian minister [Jack Murray](/wiki/John_W._Murray "John W. Murray") purchased and restored the old hotel, which was abandoned for six years, and converted it into Harvey Cedars Bible Presbyterian Conference. After ten years of directing the conference, Jack left the operations to Albert Oldham. It was run by the senior Oldham until 1995 when the director position was passed on to his son, Jon Oldham.

In 1949, the chapel was built on the property using [stained glass](/wiki/Stained_glass "Stained glass") windows purchased from the [Engleside Hotel](/wiki/Engleside_Hotel "Engleside Hotel") in [Beach Haven](/wiki/Beach_Haven,_New_Jersey "Beach Haven, New Jersey"). These windows were completely removed around 2003 after years of damage from the high winds. Many other additions and renovations took place in the following decades.

For more information on the building's history, see [Harvey Cedars Hotel](/wiki/Harvey_Cedars_Hotel "Harvey Cedars Hotel") and Camp Whelen as they occupied the same hotel during different eras.

## Namesake

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harvey_Cedars_Bible_Conference&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Namesake")]

A significant signpost in the history of the Bible Presbyterian Church was the publication and approval of what came to be known as the **Harvey Cedars Resolutions**.[[3]](#cite_note-3) In 1945 the Eighth General Synod of the BPC convened at the denomination's conference center in Harvey Cedars, New Jersey. Thus the name applied to the resolutions. With its approval, the Bible Presbyterian Church codified much of what had already characterized the denomination, namely its stance on the twin issues of [personal](/wiki/Doctrine_of_separation "Doctrine of separation") and [ecclesial separation](/wiki/Ecclesial_separation "Ecclesial separation").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

[ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [9781619962323](/wiki/Special:BookSources/9781619962323 "Special:BookSources/9781619962323").
